page: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the ESv3 family. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The system features 432.0 GB of system memory, yielding 13.5 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via an 885 GB volume, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. Virtualization is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively limited, with stress-ng single-core, bzip3 decompression single-threaded, and Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the weak category. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for both small and large block reads and large block writes remains in the average tier.

This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw compute speed. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 13.5 GB per core makes it suitable for large relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and enterprise applications. While LLM inference for small and medium models achieves average throughput, large model processing is poor, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance lies in its high memory density relative to its 32 vCPUs, allowing users to run memory-bound workloads without paying for unnecessary additional compute cores.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the ESv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz, paired with 432.0 GB of RAM and 885 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show weak single-core performance, but multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks fall consistently into the average tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 13.5 GB per core makes this instance cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute speeds. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v3 is an ESv3 family server with 32 vCPUs, 432 GB RAM, and 885 GB local storage. It offers average multi-core and memory performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v3 is an ESv3 server featuring 32 vCPUs, 432 GB RAM, and 885 GB storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense x86_64 virtual machine featuring 432 GB of RAM and 32 dedicated vCPUs for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
432.0 GB of system memory providing 13.5 GB of RAM per core
885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in benchmarks
Weak single-core performance indicating a tradeoff for high memory density
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
